name: command_run
description: Run tools as a pure batch+step command runner.

commands.description: Executable command-line instructions. During task execution, complete all currently needed steps in one batch; prefer 5 or more commands. For simple, standalone user tasks such as quick information lookups (e.g., checking current time) or single-step queries, 1-2 commands are acceptable when that fully answers the request. Include only commands whose inputs are already known when this batch is created. Independent read/search/list commands with no output dependency must share one step. Do not create test/probe commands that depend on output from earlier commands in the same batch.

command_type.description: Target internal command name from the available commands list. For namespaced tools use the flattened name shown there exactly.

command_line.description: String payload for the target command. For JSON-schema commands, pass an escaped JSON object string such as {"cmd":"rg -n query src"}. For freeform commands such as apply_patch, pass the raw freeform body. For shell-like commands, plain text is also accepted and mapped to that tool's command field.

step.description: Dependency group and execution order. Commands in the same step must have no output dependency on each other and may run together. Independent read/search/list commands with no output dependency must share one step. Commands that depend on earlier output, known work, or completed edits must use a later unique ordered step. When using compact_context, it must be the final command in the highest step.
